core_payload.content:
# Key Agents Stewardship Lane

## 1. Boundary
Key Agents Stewardship Lane owns the graph-improvement and vault-maintenance workers that read the vault, plan bounded jobs, improve structure, and prepare update-ready output. This sub-hub exists so stewardship work is legible as a full-cycle maintenance lane rather than a loose set of background utilities.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `vault-stewardship-swarm`, `capsule-decomposition-agent`, `capsule-markup-agent`, `capsule-graph-refactor-agent`, and `vault-update-agent`. Together they define the maintenance loop that inspects graph health, splits overloaded capsules, enriches markup, repairs topology, and lands reviewed vault updates.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own human conversation, mining discovery, planning compression, or branch-acceptance authority. Those belong to `user-lane`, `mining-lane`, `planning-lane`, and `governance-lane`, while background swarm activity remains under `swarm-lane`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Key Agents Stewardship Lane groups the vault-maintenance workers of N1Hub: stewardship swarm, decomposition, markup, graph refactor, and update. It makes the full-cycle graph-improvement lane explicit instead of burying maintenance inside a flat agent list. This matters because reading the vault, planning bounded jobs, repairing structure, and preparing update-ready output belong to one accountable lane whose work must stay reviewable rather than dissolving into general background activity, opaque automation, or drift.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of key-agents hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 28 outbound to ≤9.
